title: Nizam Ecosystem Membership Registry Schema
description: Validates a consumer's ecosystem-membership registry INSTANCE -- the required, machine-readable artifact that sets `n` for the 0-to-n project spectrum (NIP-0002 Stage 3; NDEBT-031), authored per the list-partition pattern of registry/scope_definition_patterns.md Section 2. Every tracked project appears as an entry in exactly one of four scope lists (in_scope / incubating / reference_archive / out_of_scope); an entry's list membership -- not a status string -- is the authoritative signal for whether it currently receives ecosystem-level governance. This schema enforces the SHAPE (the four lists exist, each is an array of entries, every entry has an identifying `name`, and out_of_scope entries record a `reason`). The exactly-one-list invariant (no `name` appears in two lists) is a relational constraint spanning sibling arrays that JSON Schema cannot express; it is enforced in code by validate.sh C12, mirroring the ecosystem_baseline same-repo-revision rule (NDEBT-023). Permits additionalProperties on entries so consumers can extend an entry (role, status, depends_on, exposes, note, last_audited, enrichment fields) without breaking validation, per schema/README.md's house convention.

entry.description: One tracked project/service/module. A minimal entry needs only an identifying `name`; a fuller entry (typical of in_scope) carries a link, a role/description, an operational status kept distinct from the list it lives in, a depends_on array, an exposes array, a dated note, and a last_audited timestamp (registry/scope_definition_patterns.md Section 2.2). Descriptive fields are open extension points.

name.description: The identifying key for this entry, unique across the whole registry (it must appear in exactly one scope list). Required on every entry; never inferred.

out_of_scope_entry.description: An entry explicitly excluded from the ecosystem. Beyond the identifying `name`, it MUST record a `reason` so the exclusion is a recorded decision rather than an omission (registry/scope_definition_patterns.md Section 2.1 / Section 5 rule 3).

name.description: The identifying key for this excluded entry.

reason.description: Why this entry is out of scope -- a recorded decision, never a bare omission.

schema_version.description: The semantic version of the registry shape this instance conforms to (a MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH string, optionally with a pre-release/build suffix). Required.

last_updated.description: The point-in-time the registry as a whole was last updated (registry/scope_definition_patterns.md Section 2). Required, and at least an ISO-8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD), optionally a full date-time. Enforced by a pattern rather than JSON Schema `format` so no FormatChecker is required.

authoritative_source.description: The repository-relative path to the authoritative copy of this registry.

dependency_map.description: An optional link or path to a separately-maintained human-readable dependency document (registry/scope_definition_patterns.md Section 3).

phase_progress.description: An optional ecosystem-wide phase/workstream progress block, distinct from any one entry's own status (registry/scope_definition_patterns.md Section 4).

in_scope.description: Actively maintained and part of the current masterplan or roadmap (registry/scope_definition_patterns.md Section 2.1). The members that set `n`.

incubating.description: Tracked but not yet a full member -- the count-0->1 state (registry/scope_definition_patterns.md Section 2.3): governance not yet bootstrapped, concept-stage, or a freshly genesis'd project awaiting its first clean Preflight/Baseline before promotion to in_scope.

reference_archive.description: No longer independently maintained, retained as a reference for one or more in_scope entries (registry/scope_definition_patterns.md Section 2.1).

out_of_scope.description: Explicitly excluded, each with a recorded `reason` (registry/scope_definition_patterns.md Section 2.1).
